article_title: Databricks Lakebase Reaches General Availability on Azure, Completing Multi-Cloud Rollout of Its Serverless PostgreSQL Database

content_preview.title: Databricks Lakebase Reaches General Availability on Azure, Completing Multi-Cloud Rollout of Its Serverless PostgreSQL Database
content_preview.summary: Databricks has made Lakebase generally available on Azure across 14 regions, completing a multi-cloud rollout of its serverless PostgreSQL database built on technology from its Neon and Mooncake Labs acquisitions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Databricks has announced the general availability of Lakebase on Microsoft Azure, making its serverless PostgreSQL database available for production workloads across 14 Azure regions worldwide. The March 2, 2026 release follows Lakebase's AWS general availability on February 22, completing a rapid multi-cloud rollout that began less than a year after Databricks acquired PostgreSQL startup Neon in June 2025.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent database infrastructure article with detailed architecture, timeline, and competitive context.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. InfoQ: VERIFIED — confirms Neon June 2025, Mooncake Labs Oct 2025, Matei Zaharia, PostgreSQL compatibility, GCP later 2026. Azure Databricks Release Notes: VERIFIED — comprehensive confirmation of all dates (Mar 2 GA, Mar 4 compliance, Mar 12 Autoscaling default, Mar 16 Apps), 14 regions (3 initial + 11 new), HA, HIPAA/C5/TISAX. Databricks blog not separately fetched but consistent claims.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against official Microsoft documentation and InfoQ reporting. Dates, regions, features, acquisitions all confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative. Good competitive context with Snowflake comparison.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Databricks Lakebase coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding database article. Primary sources verify all claims. Approved.
